name: African Style Vegetables in Coconut Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat ghee or vegetable oil over low heat in a large pan. Add the whole cloves.
2. When hot, add the onions and garlic. Sauté, stirring occasionally, for a few moments.
3. Add the carrots and potatoes. Continue to sauté for 5 minutes, stirring to prevent sticking.
4. Add the green beans. Sauté for just a few more minutes.
5. Slowly stir in the coconut milk until it creates a thick sauce; the vegetables should not be swimming in milk.
6. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat and simmer until the vegetables are cooked through (about 15-20 minutes). Stir occasionally to prevent burning.
7. Garnish with chopped cilantro.
8. Serve with Ethiopian Lentil stew or Indian dal for mixed cuisines, and bread.
If you prefer spicier flavors, add more Berbere. This dish is best made ahead of time so the flavors can meld together. Fresh parsley may be used instead of cilantro if desired.
